ratification_basis: Orchestrator spec section 0.5 (chair amendment 2026-04-28) already demoted the eval harness from a ship gate to a diagnostic instrument. A diagnostic does not require a chair-ratified contract; it requires honest provenance. This file is that provenance.
not_ratified_for: Use as a release ship gate
Use as a training or tuning signal (see open_conditions)
Promotion of ROUTING-CONTRACT.md into the orchestrator spec - that is L5 chair-only
changes_from_boardroom: g-c08: relabelled clarify -> tool (8PO - the user asked a concrete question, the val loss, which one read answers; contract rule 9). Recategorised keyword-obvious -> semantic-hard (the keyword router mis-routes it).
g-a07: kept agent. ROUTING-CONTRACT.md gained an explicit rule 7 (retry-after-failure -> agent) so the label is grounded in the contract rather than an unstated sub-rule (8TO).

g-c03: kept clarify, marked disputed: true inline. 8PO holds clarify, 8DO holds agent - a live officer split. Held at the current label pending a second rater to break the tie (Boardroom rule 3: document dissent, do not force consensus).
open_conditions: A second independent rater must relabel all 40 entries blind; report inter-rater Cohen's kappa in eval-report.json before this set is used as any training or tuning signal (8SO, 8CO).

n=40 cannot measure the spec section 9 gates at their stated sample sizes (100-example Phase 0 holdout, 5k Phase 1 set). The +10pp-vs-heuristic delta at n=40 carries an interval of roughly plus or minus 15pp - treat it as a headroom indicator, not a gate (8TO).
ROUTING-CONTRACT.md v0.1 and its authority_level < 3 clarify threshold require L5 chair ratification before promotion into the orchestrator spec (8GO, 8SO).
Grow the set toward >= 100 entries with deliberate minimal-contrast pairs (vary one of: authority_level, deny_actions, budget, history) before any default-on promotion (8TO).
citation_rule: Never publish the heuristic baseline number (currently 47.5 percent kind accuracy) without its difficulty-tier breakdown and the 'headroom on a hard-weighted set, not a router accuracy rate' frame. See EVAL-HARNESS.md.
